title: StartPointObject Property
description: Specifies the vision object to connect the starting point of the Line to. (i.e. This property defines the position of the start point of the line.)
notes:
When a Line object is first drag-and-dropped onto the Image display area of the Vision Guide Window, the default StartPointObject property is set to "Screen". (i.e. the position where the Line object is dragged to.)  However, the Line objects are normally attached to other vision objects.  This is the purpose of the StartPointObject and StartPointObject properties.  Through these 2 properties the user can define a line between any 2 vision objects (except Frames).

Frame objects cannot be used to define a start point for a Line object.  However, this does not cause a limitation because Frames are defined by other vision objects.  In these cases where you may want to define a line start point with a Frame object, simply use the vision objects which defined the Frames to define the start point of the Line Object.

It is important to note that for each specific vision sequence, only those vision objects which are executed prior to the Line object in the vision sequence steps will be available to use as an StartPointObject.
